stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 4 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 20 observations with mean 55.8 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 9.6). region B has 5 observations with mean 50.0 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 3.1). region C has 20 observations with mean 53.0 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 9.3). region D has 20 observations with mean 53.8 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 8.2). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 4.6 and 11.4 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region B: 50.1 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 50.0). Range [50.1, 50.3] as between-region SD varies from 4.6 to 11.4. Pooled mean: 53.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 53.9 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 4 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=7.6: B = 0.968.
Interval [50.1, 50.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (50.0) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (53.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
